title: Tail the Failing CI Lines
hook: Skip the full CI log and jump straight to lines that usually explain the failure.
problem: A build log is thousands of lines long and the useful error is buried near the end.
starting_state: You have a local logs/ directory from CI or a deployment run.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command reads log files and prints matching lines.
when_to_use: Use when triaging build, test, or deployment logs locally.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it as your only parser when the log format has structured fields better queried with jq or a log system.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: A short list of matching log lines with filenames, line numbers, and error text.

shorts.title: Find CI failures fast.
shorts.voiceover: When CI dumps a huge log, do not start at line one. Grep for failure language and keep the last fifty hits.
shorts.caption: grep CI logs for failure lines first.

linkedin.question: What is the first command you run when a CI log is too noisy?
linkedin.cta: Share your fastest deployment triage one-liner.

youtube.description: A safe grep one-liner for quickly finding failure lines in local CI and deployment logs.

experiments.variant_b: Find the one line that broke CI.

title: List Newest Build Artifacts
hook: Confirm what your pipeline actually produced before you deploy it.
problem: A deployment references an artifact, but you are not sure which files were built most recently.
starting_state: You have an artifacts/ directory with build outputs.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command prints artifact timestamps, sizes, and paths.
when_to_use: Use before deploys, rollbacks, or artifact handoffs.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it for remote artifact stores unless they are mounted locally.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: The newest artifact files sorted by timestamp.

shorts.title: What did CI build?
shorts.voiceover: Before deploying, list the newest artifact files with timestamps and sizes. It is a fast sanity check.
shorts.caption: Verify build artifacts before deploy.

linkedin.question: Do you verify artifacts before deploy, or trust the pipeline name?
linkedin.cta: Save this for your next release check.

youtube.description: Check the newest local build artifacts with find and sort.

title: Check the Current Release Symlink
hook: One glance tells you which release directory production is pointing at.
problem: A deploy uses a current symlink and you need to verify the active release.
starting_state: A releases/ directory contains a current symlink.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command resolves and displays the symlink.
when_to_use: Use after deploys, rollbacks, or suspicious version mismatches.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it if your platform does not use release directories or symlinks.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: The absolute path of the active release and the symlink metadata.

shorts.title: Which release is live?
shorts.voiceover: If deploys use a current symlink, readlink shows the real active release path immediately.
shorts.caption: Verify the live release symlink.

linkedin.question: Does your deploy system make the active release obvious?
linkedin.cta: Use this before blaming the app code.

youtube.description: Check a release-directory current symlink with readlink and ls.

title: Find the Largest CI Logs
hook: Huge logs often point to loops, noisy tests, or runaway debug output.
problem: A CI job is slow or hard to inspect because some logs are unexpectedly large.
starting_state: You have local CI or deployment logs under logs/.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command reports the largest log files.
when_to_use: Use when log uploads are slow or a job output is unusually noisy.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it for compressed logs when you need uncompressed size analysis.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: The 10 largest log files with byte counts.

shorts.title: Find noisy CI logs.
shorts.voiceover: Sort logs by size first. The biggest file is often where the pipeline is wasting your attention.
shorts.caption: Largest logs first.

linkedin.question: Have oversized logs ever hidden the real failure from your team?
linkedin.cta: Try sorting logs by size during your next CI cleanup.

youtube.description: Spot unusually large CI logs with find and sort.

title: Show Release Directory Ages
hook: See your newest release directories without opening a dashboard.
problem: You need to confirm recent releases exist and identify their order.
command: find releases/ -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -printf '%T@ %TY-%Tm-%Td %TH:%TM %p\n' | sort -nr | head -10 | cut -d' ' -f2-
starting_state: A releases/ directory contains timestamped release folders.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command lists release directories by modification time.
when_to_use: Use during deploy checks, rollback planning, or release cleanup reviews.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it if directory modification time is not meaningful in your deploy process.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: The most recently modified release directories.

commands: find releases/ -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d -printf '%T@ %TY-%Tm-%Td %TH:%TM %p\n' | sort -nr | head -10 | cut -d' ' -f2-

shorts.title: Newest releases first.
shorts.voiceover: When releases are directories, sort them by modification time to see the newest deploy candidates.
shorts.caption: List recent release directories.

linkedin.question: How do you confirm the release directory you expect is actually present?
linkedin.cta: Add this to your deployment triage notes.

youtube.description: List recent release directories by modification time.

title: Extract Environment Names Only
hook: Audit environment labels without printing secret values.
problem: You need to see which environment names appear in config files, but must avoid exposing secrets.
starting_state: Config or deployment files exist in the current project directory.
what_changed: Nothing changes. Only matching environment variable names are printed.
when_to_use: Use when checking env naming consistency across deployment files.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it when you need values; this command intentionally prints names only.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Unique environment variable names such as APP_ENV, NODE_ENV, or RAILS_ENV.

shorts.title: Env names, not secrets.
shorts.voiceover: For a quick audit, print only environment variable names. Do not dump values into chat or screenshots.
shorts.caption: Audit env names safely.

linkedin.question: Do your deployment checks avoid printing secret values by default?
linkedin.cta: Use name-only checks when sharing terminal output.

youtube.description: List environment variable names without exposing values.

title: Smoke Check an HTTP Status
hook: A deploy is not done until the endpoint answers.
problem: You need a quick status-code check after a deployment.
starting_state: You have a health endpoint URL and network access from the machine running the check.
what_changed: Nothing changes locally. The command sends one HTTP GET request.
when_to_use: Use after deployments, restarts, or DNS changes.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it for endpoints where a GET request has side effects.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only locally.
expected_output: A status code and total request time, such as 200 0.183s.

shorts.title: Deploy smoke check.
shorts.voiceover: Use curl to print just the HTTP status and timing. It is the fastest post-deploy heartbeat.
shorts.caption: Status code plus timing.

linkedin.question: What is your minimum smoke check after deployment?
linkedin.cta: Post the one command your team trusts.

youtube.description: Check HTTP status and response time after deployment.

title: Compare Artifact Checksums
hook: Verify two artifact copies match before blaming deployment code.
problem: You need to confirm whether a built artifact and deployed artifact are identical.
starting_state: You have two artifact files available on disk.
what_changed: Nothing changes. Checksums are calculated and printed.
when_to_use: Use when checking whether a deployed artifact matches the built artifact.
when_not_to_use: Do not run it on huge files if hashing would compete with production I/O during an incident.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Two SHA-256 hashes and filenames; matching hashes mean matching file contents.

shorts.title: Do these artifacts match?
shorts.voiceover: When a deploy looks wrong, hash the built artifact and the deployed artifact. Same hash, same bytes.
shorts.caption: Compare deployed artifact hashes.

linkedin.question: Do you verify artifact identity during deployment incidents?
linkedin.cta: Keep checksum checks in your rollback playbook.

youtube.description: Compare build and deployed artifacts with sha256sum.

experiments.variant_b: Check the artifact before the app.

title: Count Failures by Test File
hook: Turn noisy test logs into a ranked failure list.
problem: A CI log has repeated failures and you need to see which files appear most often.
command: grep -RhoE '[A-Za-z0-9_./-]+\.(test|spec)\.(js|ts|py|rb)' logs/ | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | head
starting_state: CI logs mention failing test file paths.
what_changed: Nothing changes. The command extracts and counts file path mentions.
when_to_use: Use when a test suite produces many repeated failure lines.
when_not_to_use: Do not use it when your CI emits structured JSON test reports that should be queried directly.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: A ranked list of test file paths with occurrence counts.

commands: grep -RhoE '[A-Za-z0-9_./-]+\.(test|spec)\.(js|ts|py|rb)' logs/ | sort | uniq -c | sort -nr | head

shorts.title: Rank failing tests.
shorts.voiceover: Extract test filenames from the log, count them, and sort. Now the noisy failure has a shape.
shorts.caption: Count repeated failing test files.

linkedin.question: When many tests fail, do you rank the failures or inspect them in log order?
linkedin.cta: Try counting repeated test paths first.

youtube.description: Rank test file mentions in CI logs with grep, sort, and uniq.

title: Inspect Release Disk Usage
hook: Disk pressure during deploys often starts in old release directories.
problem: A host is low on disk and release directories may be taking too much space.
starting_state: A releases/ directory contains release folders.
what_changed: Nothing changes. Disk usage is calculated and printed.
when_to_use: Use when deploys fail due to disk space or cleanup policies need review.
when_not_to_use: Do not run recursive disk scans on extremely busy hosts if the extra I/O is unacceptable.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: The largest release directories, sorted by human-readable size.

shorts.title: Find heavy releases.
shorts.voiceover: When deploys hit disk pressure, check release directory sizes before deleting anything.
shorts.caption: Inspect release disk usage safely.

linkedin.question: What usually fills disk first on your deployment hosts?
linkedin.cta: Measure before cleanup, especially during incidents.

youtube.description: Inspect release directory disk usage during deployment incidents.

title: Check Image Tags in Manifests
hook: Find the image tags your deployment files reference without printing env values.
problem: You need to confirm which container image tags are present in deployment manifests.
starting_state: Deployment YAML or manifest files exist under deploy/.
what_changed: Nothing changes. Matching image lines are printed.
when_to_use: Use before or after a deploy to confirm expected image references.
when_not_to_use: Do not rely on it if manifests are templated and the rendered output is the real source of truth.
undo: No undo needed because this command is read-only.
expected_output: Unique image references such as image: registry.example/app:v1.2.3.

shorts.title: Which image will deploy?
shorts.voiceover: Grep deployment manifests for image lines to confirm the tag before rollout or rollback.
shorts.caption: Check image tags in deploy files.

linkedin.question: Have you ever deployed the right code with the wrong image tag?
linkedin.cta: Add a manifest tag check to your release routine.

youtube.description: Confirm container image tags in deployment manifests.
